back: The natural process by which life emerges from nonliving chemistry.
He ruled out ordinary spontaneous generation in modern conditions, sharpening the origin-of-life problem.

front: Two core features of life in this module
back: Replicable information and a metabolism that can sustain replication.
DNA needs proteins to function, but proteins are encoded by DNA.
Abiotic conditions can produce amino acids, key molecular building blocks.
Carbon makes stable yet versatile bonds with itself and common elements like hydrogen, oxygen, and nitrogen.
An early stage where RNA-like molecules handled both information storage and catalytic function.
More stable and replicable molecules persist longer and become more common before true cells exist.
A primitive cell-like compartment, often modeled with self-assembled fatty-acid membranes.
They concentrate reactants, protect fragile molecules, and enable sustained chemical networks.
When replication, metabolism, and compartmental boundaries reinforce self-maintenance and reproduction.
Origin-of-life has strong plausible pathways but no single fully confirmed complete sequence yet.
